title: EscalationEvent
description: Durable record emitted whenever a reject verdict has reason_code in {policy_breach, hook_failure} (FR-007, Principle VI — Human-in-the-Loop Escalation, NON-NEGOTIABLE). Schema-violation rejects do NOT escalate (those are client bugs). The raw payload is NEVER embedded; only a SHA-256 digest is kept to avoid leaking PII into the audit log.

kind.description: Discriminator used by the JSONL audit log reader.

correlation_id.description: Joins this escalation to the verdict, the StructuredError, and the model turn.

policy_snapshot_version.description: Pinned policy snapshot — resolves the concurrent-policy-update edge case unambiguously.

summary.description: One-sentence human-readable description, e.g. 'Refund of $750 blocked by policy refund-policy v3.'

actions_taken.description: Always empty on a pre-call block — the whole point of FR-006 is that no action was taken. Kept for shape parity with Principle VI's typed escalation payload.

escalation_reason.description: Narrower than HookVerdict.reason_code — matches the two causes that actually escalate to a human reviewer.

routing_target.description: Copied from PolicyConfig.escalation_pathway for policy_breach; an internal route for hook_failure.

rejected_payload_digest.description: SHA-256 hex digest of the JSON-serialized ToolCallPayload. Raw payload is deliberately excluded to avoid leaking PII into the audit log.
